sanskrit:
समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः।
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः॥
तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येन केनचित्।
अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्तिमान्मे प्रियो नरः॥
transliteration: samaḥ śatrau ca mitre ca tathā mānāpamānayoḥ | śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣu samaḥ saṅga-vivarjitaḥ || tulya-nindā-stutir maunī santuṣṭo yena kenacit | aniketaḥ sthira-matir bhaktimān me priyo naraḥ ||
literal_meaning: Equal toward foe and friend, likewise to honor and dishonor; same in cold-heat, pleasure-pain, fame-infamy; free from clinging; content with whatever; homeless yet mind-steady; devoted—such a person is dear to Me.
contextual_meaning: Krishna describes the emotionally invulnerable, non-attached devotee whose nervous system no longer tags experiences as “good” or “bad,” thus sustaining an inner homeostasis that makes every external change psychologically neutral.
psychological_state: High vagal-tone, meta-cognitive awareness; ego functions as observer, not claimant; implicit memory patterns de-conditioned from dualistic appraisal.
trigger_scenario: Your post is roasted on social media, your best friend ghosts you, and your AC dies in a heat-wave—yet you sleep fine, wake up calm, and still show up on time to volunteer at the shelter.
anchor_text: Stay steady through praise or blame, pleasure or pain, like the sky unshaken by storms.
